purpose: Help the PPT AI Agent choose a broad red-blue comparison canvas first, then compose the final page in slides/*.tsx from reusable components based on the page plan and grounded content.

source_theme.relationship_to_v1: This canvas group keeps the v1 component library and theme, but does not copy v1's concrete country/economy/demographic page blueprints.

rules: Do not treat canvas files as finished slide structures.
Copy or derive the selected canvas into ./slides before adapting it for a concrete deck.
Use each canvas only as a branded frame before composing real content.
group.json.layouts and manifest.json source.path must only reference this directory.
Final deck pages should replace canvas slot guides with page-specific component compositions.

components.role: Reusable red-blue comparison component library copied from the red-blue comparison v1 component set.
rules: Choose component families from page semantics, not visual convenience.
Prefer composing existing components before adding new ones.

data.role: Content data for demo or concrete decks.
rules: JSON carries content only, not page-structure decisions.
If layout or component choice is wrong, edit slides/*.tsx.

theme.role: Shared color, typography, and visual token definitions.

blueprints.name: Cover Canvas
blueprints.composition_guidance: Keep the hero comparison title and entity legend if they support the story. Replace the visual slot with a relevant image, small chart motif, evidence cue, or concise scope component.

blueprints.name: Content Canvas
blueprints.composition_guidance: Use this as the default frame when the page plan does not demand a dedicated comparison lane or primary chart. Fill the body with cards, headings, metrics, images, tables, or short narrative blocks.

blueprints.name: Comparison Canvas
blueprints.composition_guidance: Treat each lane as a scaffold, not as a fixed card. Replace lane guides with EntitySnapshotCard, ComparativeMetricRow, ComparisonTablePanel, StrategicInsightCard, or custom lane-level compositions.

blueprints.name: Chart Evidence Canvas
suitable_for: table or source note alongside a visual
avoid_for: pages with no primary visual anchor
blueprints.composition_guidance: Use ChartContainer plus a chart module for quantitative evidence, or ImageShowcasePanel for one visual asset. Use the right rail for concise interpretation, KPI rows, source notes, or a compact comparison table.

blueprints.name: Closing Canvas
blueprints.composition_guidance: Keep density low. Replace or adapt the contact module with final CTA, next-step cards, or concise follow-up information while preserving editable text.

selection_guide: Choose cover-canvas only for the opening page or an intentional title reset.
Choose content-canvas for ordinary pages where the Agent needs freedom to compose cards, text, image, table, or mixed modules.
Choose comparison-canvas when the page intent is explicitly to compare two or three entities, options, markets, products, or capabilities.
Choose chart-evidence-canvas when one chart, table, image, screenshot, map, or other evidence asset should dominate the page and interpretation should sit beside it.

If the page is a timeline or evidence page, start from chart-evidence-canvas when there is one primary evidence anchor; start from content-canvas when the timeline/evidence structure needs a custom full-width composition.
Choose closing-canvas only for the final thank-you, contact, or next-step page.
agent_workflow_notes: This template group intentionally provides canvas-level starting points, not finished industry pages.
Start by choosing the broad canvas family from page intent; do not start by copying a v1 business blueprint.
Before editing slides/*.tsx, read components/README.md and identify the component families that fit the page plan.
Derive final pages in slides/*.tsx, then replace slot guides with concrete component composition.
Use data/demo or deck JSON for content values only; layout, slot ownership, and component choice belong in TSX.
Do not paste source HTML or a concrete business page into a canvas. Rebuild the page from red-blue components so it remains editable and PPT-friendly.
